summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is resonance?

Resonance Resonance occurs when a system is driven at its natural frequency — amplitude increases dramatically. The other options — 'When waves cancel', 'When frequency approaches zero', and 'When amplitude decreases' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

summary_sections.title: Special Topics
summary_sections.content_html:
Important Concepts

Fundamental frequency for a string fixed at both ends: L =

Harmonics Fundamental (1st harmonic) : Lowest frequency; L = λ/2 (string fixed at both ends). 2nd harmonic : L = λ. nth harmonic : L = nλ/2. f_n = nf₁ = nv/(2L) for a string fixed at both ends. The other options — 'λ', '2λ', and 'λ/4' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.
